incorrects.description: Student selected 3 — confused Dan's value (3) with the difference between Ava and Dan

steps.text: You found Dan's number. But we need the difference—how many more Ava has than Dan. Compare their bar heights.

incorrects.description: Student selected 8 — confused Ava's value (8) with the difference between Ava and Dan

steps.text: You found Ava's number. But the question asks how many more—we need to find the difference between Ava's 8 and Dan's 3.

incorrects.description: Student selected 11 — added Ava's value and Dan's value (8 + 3) instead of finding the difference

steps.text: When we ask 'how many more,' we subtract to find the difference. Look at Ava's bar and Dan's bar—how much taller is Ava's?

incorrects.description: Student answered incorrectly three or more times — system models the answer

params.description: Animation highlights Ava's bar at 8 and Dan's bar at 3, then visually shows the 5-unit difference between them

steps.text: Let me show you how this works. Ava read 8 books—her bar reaches to 8. Dan read 3 books—his bar reaches to 3. To find how many more, we subtract: 8 minus 3 equals 5. Ava read 5 more books than Dan.
